key_insight: A frozen CT-CLIP foundation model, paired with a lightweight survival head, can improve multimodal lung cancer survival prediction using limited patient data.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help stratify pretreatment lung cancer patients into higher- and lower-risk groups to support prognosis-informed treatment planning.
method: Used pretreatment CT images plus clinical variables from 242 lung cancer patients.
Compared frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, and low-rank adaptation.
Benchmarked against clinical-only and other multimodal baselines with modality ablations.
evidence_signal: In a 242-patient cohort, the frozen CT-CLIP setup outperformed the clinical baseline and was comparable to or better than other multimodal approaches.
watch_item: The study is based on a relatively small, curated cohort, so broader validation is needed before routine clinical implementation.
paper_title: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
